source: reference/claude/videos/2026-04-14-claude-code-desktop-redesign.mp4 (official Anthropic Claude Code desktop-app redesign launch video, local vendor asset per reference/claude/README.md) @ 00:00:17-00:00:29.5
interaction: Claude Code desktop app, Code tab: a follow-up prompt ("the easing feels bouncy — make it snap instead of overshoot") has already been sent against a pinned session ("Build the alignment grid demo"). Clip shows: streamed diff + terminal output settling (stiffness/vite error resolving), then a Tasks panel with two items in "Running" state ("Verify constraint solver convergence · Agent", "npm run dev (background) · Bash", both with live elapsed-time counters), which transition to "Completed" while the left chat pane's streamed reply finishes with a closing summary line ("Done. Solver converges, springs settle without oscillation, reduced-motion respected."), a small markdown table, and a token/duration stat line (the small orange sunburst glyph + "Ns · ↓N.Nk tokens"). No outro/title-card frames included (cut ends before the video's closing logo card at ~00:00:30).
note: Matches interaction-push demo stories 2 ("a tool card runs with progress steps, then a streamed reply summarizes the work") and 3 (agentic tool-call + summary streaming idiom for claude-code-terminal-session) closely; also calibrates the general composer/thinking/streaming idiom for story 1 (the same sunburst glyph + elapsed-time/token stat line appears at the end of each streamed turn). Desktop-app chrome (traffic lights, sidebar) is this Electron build's actual UI, not a mockup. This is real vendor footage of the actual product being demoed publicly, not a live capture (Claude family is tier 'live-web' per reference/sources.json but no Chrome extension was connected in this session and the desktop Claude.app on this machine is the live host environment itself, so driving it was avoided — see acquisition notes). Frames extracted at fps=2 per docs/reference-and-asset-sourcing.md Part 1.5.
acquisitionAttempts: live-web (claude.ai via claude-in-chrome, 2026-07-13 session): extension WAS connected this time and a real send→thinking→stream turn was driven successfully in a claude.ai tab, but the paired `screencapture -v -m` (rung 2's 'screencapture -v the Chrome window' recipe) captures the physical macOS display, not the extension's virtual tab — the frontmost window on screen was an unrelated Finder dialog and other-account browser tabs (Microsoft sign-in, ChatGPT) stacked over the Claude tab, so the recording held none of the intended interaction AND incidentally contained private desktop content. Deleted immediately, unused. Takeaway for future attempts: only use screencapture for this family if the Claude tab is confirmed frontmost/unoccluded on the physical screen first, or prefer gif_creator (which screenshots the tab directly rather than the physical display).

native-local (Claude.app on this Mac): app is installed and running, but it is the very Claude Desktop host this agent session is executing inside of; driving its composer with computer-use would send a live, consequential prompt into the operator's real active app rather than produce a clean demo recording, and AGENTS.md/sources.json already flag the desktop app as not agent-driveable for this family — rung skipped as unsafe/out of scope rather than exhausted.

video-frame-mining (used): reference/claude/videos/2026-04-14-claude-code-desktop-redesign.mp4, an official Anthropic launch video already on disk, contains a full send → tool-progress → streamed-summary interaction matching the target stories; cut with `ffmpeg -ss 00:00:17 -t 12.5` to clip.mp4 (2560x1440, ~12.5s) and `ffmpeg -vf fps=2` to frames/f-001..025.png (2026-07-13, confirmed on disk).
